Building a Private AI Platform on Red Hat OpenShift AI

A GitOps-managed, GPU-accelerated, enterprise-grade AI infrastructure — assembled stage by stage.

GitOps-Managed GPU-Accelerated Enterprise-Grade

RHOAI Demo · Platform Architecture · Red Hat

The Challenge

Enterprise AI Stalls Without Governed Infrastructure

  • Fragmented tooling — every team builds its own ML stack
  • Ungoverned GPU — no quota, no scheduling, no audit trail
  • Credentials and data leaving the cluster perimeter
  • No reproducibility — model deployments drift from source
  • Compliance gap — who deployed what, when, and with which data?
The Approach

GitOps-Driven, Stage-by-Stage Platform Assembly

Five stages build incrementally. Each stage is a self-contained Argo CD Application — auditable, repeatable, and rollback-ready from a single Git commit.

110 · Base Platform 120 · GPU Service 210 · Model Serving 220 · MaaS 230 · Private RAG

"Ship the platform, not the PowerPoint."

Stage 110 · Base Platform

A Stable Foundation Changes Everything

  • RHOAI 3.4 operator — single DataScienceCluster CR owns all components
  • ODF Multicloud Object Gateway — S3-compatible model & pipeline artifact store
  • Argo CD GitOps — annotation-tracked, project-scoped Applications
  • RBAC baseline — platform-admin, data-scientist, model-consumer roles
  • Model Registry — versioned model lineage from day one

"You cannot bypass the boring work of standardization."

Stage 120 · GPU as a Service

GPU Becomes a Governed Service

  • Node Feature Discovery — auto-detect GPU model, VRAM, CUDA version
  • NVIDIA GPU Operator — driver lifecycle, device plugin, DCGM metrics
  • Kueue quota management — per-namespace GPU budgets, fair sharing
  • Hardware Profiles — developer self-service via curated GPU tiers
  • Result: GPU allocation auditable, schedulable, and budget-governed
Stage 210 · Model Serving Foundation

From Accelerator to Model Endpoint

  • KServe single-model serving with vLLM runtime
  • Nemotron 3 Nano 30B (4-bit AWQ) on a single L4 GPU
  • GuideLLM benchmark — validated throughput under load
  • Grafana dashboards — TTFT, ITL, queue depth, token throughput
~67ms
TTFT P50
~5ms
ITL P50
vLLM
Serving Runtime
1× L4
GPU (24 GB)
Stage 220 · Models as a Service

Models as a Governed Shared Service

  • MaaS Gateway API — unified model endpoint contract
  • Kuadrant AuthPolicy — per-consumer API key auth & rate limiting
  • External GPT-4o-mini proxy — same governance, external inference
  • GenAI Playground — interactive model evaluation for developers
  • API key management — issue, rotate, revoke without redeployment
Stage 230 · Private Data RAG

Private Data Stays Private

  • Llama Stack / OGX — retrieval-augmented generation orchestration
  • RHOAI product docs corpus — ~500 pages, structured metadata
  • Docling pipelines — PDF/HTML to structured chunks
  • pgvector — embeddings with version, chapter, product metadata
  • AutoRAG optimization — tuned chunking & retrieval parameters
  • Zero data egress — all retrieval and generation on-cluster
Architecture

Full Platform Stack

Developer Surfaces GenAI Playground · Workbenches · AI Pipelines
Private Data RAG (Stage 230) Llama Stack · pgvector · Docling · AutoRAG
Models as a Service (Stage 220) Gateway API · Kuadrant Auth · API Keys
Model Serving Foundation (Stage 210) KServe · vLLM · Nemotron · Grafana
GPU as a Service (Stage 120) NFD · NVIDIA Operator · Kueue · HW Profiles
RHOAI Base Platform (Stage 110) RHOAI 3.4 · ODF MCG · Argo CD · RBAC
OpenShift Container Platform 4.20 Compute · Networking · Security · Storage

What's Next

From Demo to Production

  • Model customization — LoRA fine-tuning via RHOAI Training Operator
  • Guardrails & safety — NeMo Guardrails for content policy enforcement
  • Distributed inference — llm-d for multi-GPU / multi-node serving
  • Edge deployment — inference at the data source
  • Model evaluation pipelines — automated quality gates in CI/CD

Each extension follows the same GitOps stage pattern — auditable, repeatable, rollback-ready.

Thank You

Building Private AI — one governed stage at a time.
